steps: Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/agent-context.ps1.
Read engine/agent/manifest.json.
Create or update games/<game_name>/game.agent.json.

For AI Game Design Spec v1 generated 2D/3D package work, write or update fail-closed game.agent.json aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec rows before implementation so gameplay family, template, camera, input map, core loop, scene list, asset requests, systems, package targets, validation recipe ids, quality gates, and unsupported claims reference only same-manifest package targets and validation recipes.

For AI Safe Content Mutation Ledger v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.contentMutationLedger rows fail-closed with reviewed mutation ownership, forbidden shared paths, and explicit package/CMake registration surfaces before writing generated game content.

For AI Placeholder Asset Pipeline v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.placeholderAssetPipeline rows limited to first-party placeholder assets, deterministic replacementWorkflow rows, package handoff counters, and no external asset downloads.

For AI Generated Game Playtest Loop v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGamePlaytestLoop rows tied to reviewed validation recipe surfaces, failure classification, and no validation weakening.

For AI Validation Remediation Recipes v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.validationRemediationRecipes rows scoped to common missing package file, invalid reference, host gate, shader/tool gap, counter mismatch, and runtime package load failures.

For ai-generated-game-quality-rubric-v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGameQualityRubric rows objective and evidence-based around objective, controls, feedback, deterministic-package-smoke, budget-evidence, unsupportedRows, and broad production readiness exclusions.

CI Matrix Contract Check v1 keeps ciMatrixCheck and classifyPrValidationTier aligned with tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, tools/classify-pr-validation-tier.ps1, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, C++23 Release Package Artifact CI Evidence v1, Full Repository Static Analysis CI Contract v1, Assert-ReleasePackageArtifacts, Windows/Linux/sanitizer/macOS/iOS lane selection, static-analysis, and broader build/package CI execution evidence.
Use only public mirakana:: APIs from engine headers.

Use MK_core diagnostics/profile/trace export/review/reconstruction APIs for deterministic CPU observability; host adapters can attach a DiagnosticsRecorder and optional IProfileClock through DesktopHostServices to capture runtime_host.frame and renderer counters; use summarize_counter_budget and summarize_profile_budget for named nearest-rank count/min/average/p95/p99/max budget summaries with missing-sample diagnostics, invalid non-finite counter diagnostics, invalid-threshold diagnostics, and threshold status before optimization claims; use build_diagnostics_ops_plan plus export_diagnostics_trace_json, review_diagnostics_trace_json, and import_diagnostics_trace_json for dependency-free summary/trace/crash-review/telemetry handoff and first-party exported Trace Event JSON review/reconstruction instead of inventing game-local profiling formats, and do not expose native/backend handles.

Use MK_core ScratchArena, ScratchLease, ScratchLeaseStatus, and scratch_lease_status_label when a generated or sample game needs bounded frame temporary or per-worker scratch buffers through non-owning std::span<std::byte> leases from stable non-copyable/non-movable arena owners, explicit reset_at_safe_point boundaries, owner-checked worker releases, padding-aware capacity/high-water evidence, and memory_counter_row export. Use MK_core Memory Diagnostics v1 APIs when a generated or sample game needs memory evidence: MemoryCounterRow, MemoryLifetimeClass, MemoryDiagnosticsOptions, MemoryDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryBudgetPressure, MemoryDiagnosticsCode, MemoryDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_memory_diagnostics, and label helpers aggregate bytes/counts, reuse counts, reset counts, high-water bytes, budget pressure, stale-generation diagnostics, use-after-safe-point diagnostics, cross-thread-free diagnostics, and false-sharing diagnostics by lifetime class. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-memory-diagnostics maps selected package resident CPU bytes, resident GPU bytes/budget, upload/staging bytes, transient GPU allocation counters, and framegraph aliasing-barrier evidence into package-visible memory_diagnostics_* rows including memory_diagnostics_status=ready, memory_diagnostics_ready=1, memory_diagnostics_diagnostics=0, positive memory_diagnostics_total_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_package_resident_cpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_upload_staging_bytes, memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_pressure=nominal, positive memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_aliasing_barriers, and memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_framegraph_aliasing_ready=1 when multi-queue evidence is requested. Treat these rows as focused core scratch and diagnostics evidence only; they do not prove global allocator hooks/enforcement, broad pool policy, all-core CPU scheduling, NUMA/affinity, SIMD dispatch, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, automatic/LRU GPU residency, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Scheduling Evidence v1 Phase 3 APIs as host-independent scheduler evidence rows, deterministic bounded queue execution evidence, and selected package-visible scheduler evidence: JobWorkerTopologyRow, JobQueueCounterRow, JobSchedulingWorkItemRow, JobSchedulingExecutionOptions, JobSchedulingExecutionOrderRow, JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsSummary, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsCode, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_job_scheduling_diagnostics, build_job_scheduling_execution_evidence, job_scheduling_diagnostics_code_label, and job_scheduling_diagnostics_status_label. Generated and sample games may report worker topology, explicit dependency ids, worker-local output counts, deterministic merge order, bounded queue capacity, queue depth high-water, submitted/completed jobs, steal attempts/successes, worker waits, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, deterministic/nondeterministic merge counts, queue_rows, worker_scratch_rows, execution_order, per-worker MemoryCounterRow scratch charges, scratch bytes/high-water, scratch misuse, undersized job batches, and oversized job batches through these value rows. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-scheduling-evidence package smoke reports job_scheduling_evidence_status=ready, job_scheduling_evidence_ready=1, clean diagnostics, two workers, two queue rows, three submitted/completed/executed jobs, three deterministic merges, positive scratch byte/high-water counters, and zero native-thread/thread-pool/affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap side-effect flags. Generated games must still hand off requests for actual all-core CPU scheduling beyond JobExecutionPool v1, work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Execution Worker Pool v1 APIs when a generated game, sample, or host adapter needs real bounded CPU worker execution with deterministic evidence: JobExecutionPool, JobExecutionPoolDesc, JobExecutionBatchDesc, JobExecutionTaskDesc, JobExecutionContext, JobExecutionRunResult, JobExecutionPoolStatus, JobExecutionRunStatus, JobExecutionDiagnosticCode, execute(batch), request_stop, stop_and_drain, worker_threads_started, and job_execution_* label helpers. The pool starts explicit std::jthread workers, drains execute(batch) before returning, passes worker-local ScratchArena and std::stop_token into each task, reports actual worker_threads_started, catches exceptions, rejects invalid tasks and queue/dependency hazards, and maps deterministic publish evidence into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence. Generated games must still hand off requests for work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, package-visible job_execution_foundation_* counters, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.
Do not use Dear ImGui, SDL3, editor, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs for game runtime HUDs or menus; target first-party mirakana::ui contracts.

When a generated or sample game needs a reusable unsupported engine capability, stop at AI Engine Capability Handoff v1: use mirakana::EngineCapabilityHandoffRequestRow plus mirakana::review_engine_capability_handoff_request and optional game.agent.json aiWorkflow.engineCapabilityHandoffs descriptor rows to record the canonical capability id, blocked feature, current workaround, affected game files, desired first-party public contract, and required evidence without editing engine internals, exposing native handles, or leaking SDL3/Dear ImGui/renderer/RHI/backend/middleware contracts.

When authored source assets and Scene v2 data need a host-independent runtime scene package, use the validated authored-to-runtime workflow register-source-asset -> cook-registered-source-assets -> migrate-scene-v2-runtime-package -> validate-runtime-scene-package. Select validate-runtime-scene-package inputs from game.agent.json runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows after the package exists, and stop there unless a selected renderer or desktop recipe adds its own host-gated validation.

Register headless/default games with MK_add_game in games/CMakeLists.txt; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimePackage for config-only desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage for first-party cooked-scene desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime2DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 2D gameplay with cooked sprite/material/audio/orthographic scene payloads, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime3DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 3D gameplay with cooked texture/mesh/morph_mesh_cpu/material/animation_float_clip/animation_quaternion_clip/perspective scene/physics-collision payloads plus transform/morph/quaternion scene-transform package smoke, selected safe-point package streaming smoke, selected renderer quality package smoke, selected scene collision package smoke, and primary camera/controller package smoke, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage only when a scaffold needs source material/HLSL authoring inputs plus host-built desktop shader artifacts, or MK_add_desktop_runtime_game with GAME_MANIFEST plus mirakana::Win32DesktopGameHost only when selecting the optional desktop runtime lane, and declare config/assets/cooked package payloads in runtimePackageFiles with PACKAGE_FILES_FROM_MANIFEST so they ship beside the executable. Cooked-scene, packaged-2D, and packaged-3D manifests must also declare runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows with safe game-relative .geindex paths and explicit scene asset keys for validate-runtime-scene-package, plus host-gated-safe-point packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows that reference those validation targets and .geindex package files for selected safe-point package streaming execution. DesktopRuntime2DPackage manifests that opt into reviewed sandbox authoring must declare sandboxWorldAuthoringTargets rows for tile definitions, palette brushes, chunk templates, procedural seeds, changed files, tilemap package changed files, and dependency edges; source authoring review documents stay out of runtimePackageFiles while --require-sandbox-authoring-review validates package-visible counters and zero external image decoding, downloads, importer plugin execution, native handles, renderer/RHI residency, runtime source parsing, and package apply during review. Source-tree desktop runtime builds stage registered package files and shader artifacts beside a target-specific executable directory to avoid cross-target generated payload collisions, and generated 2D/material-shader/3D package targets get DXC-available source-tree shader-artifact CTests before installed package validation when they declare selected D3D12 shader artifacts. Source material and HLSL files remain authoring inputs and must not be listed in runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, or packageStreamingResidencyTargets. Use the editor Apply Package Registration button or tools/register-runtime-package-files.ps1 -GameManifest games/<game_name>/game.agent.json -RuntimePackageFile <game-relative-file> after reviewing package draft rows, then run schema/package validation. Use literal CMake PACKAGE_FILES only when intentionally mirroring the manifest.

Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/prepare-worktree.ps1 after entering a manual linked worktree so ignored worktree roots, external/vcpkg, and any existing local vcpkg_installed package tree are ready before configure. Register auto-merge with gh pr merge --auto --merge --match-head-commit <headRefOid> and leave branch deletion to cleanup because gh pr merge --delete-branch also deletes the local branch. After a task-owned PR is merged into main, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> -BaseRef origin/main -BaseBranch main -Remote origin -DeleteLocalBranch -DeleteRemoteBranch from Local or another non-target checkout for guarded post-merge cleanup; it fetches/prunes origin, requires the local checkout to be clean and on the base branch, fast-forwards it to origin/main, verifies local and remote branch ancestry, then removes only clean merged worktrees. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-toolchain.ps1 when diagnosing local CMake, CTest, CPack, clang-format, Visual Studio, MSBuild, or PATH issues; toolchain-check also enforces that visible CMake configure/build presets inherit normalized-configure-environment / normalized-build-environment so raw cmake --preset commands inherit PATH and unset Path, repository wrappers collapse parent PATH/Path variants into one child Path on Windows and PATH elsewhere for CMake/MSBuild/CTest, and reports linked-worktree vcpkg readiness; treat generated out/build/<preset> trees as disposable outputs and do not repair them by editing CMakeCache.txt, .vcxproj, .sln, or MSBuild props; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 and tools/ctest.ps1 for focused local CMake/CTest loops; tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; MSVC targets use bounded /MP2 plus /Zf through MK_apply_common_target_options; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism; reserve raw cmake --preset checks plus check-toolchain.ps1 -RequireDirectCMake for explicit direct-command requirements; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-format.ps1 / pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/format.ps1 for repository formatting because raw clang-format --dry-run commands require direct-clang-format-status=ready or an explicit resolved tool path, and those wrappers also check/apply tracked text normalization (LF, UTF-8 without BOM, a single final newline, and no trailing EOF blank lines); use tools/check-text-format.ps1 / tools/format-text.ps1 for text-only loops; run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1 before completion; validate.ps1 runs independent static checks through bounded parallel jobs with per-check timeouts before the sequential build, clang-tidy smoke, and CTest gate, writes complete per-script logs under ignored out/validation-logs/ while keeping console output bounded, accepts -SkipTidySmoke only when another selected lane owns full static-analysis evidence, accepts -StaticJobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit static-check throttle, and accepts -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds <N> only for host-specific timeout diagnostics. Hosted workflow checkouts stay SHA-pinned with permissions: contents: read and persist-credentials: false, then retry once after transient fetch/auth failure before toolchain steps; ccache stats are guarded so early checkout/toolchain failures keep the primary diagnostic visible. Hosted CI uses validate.ps1 -StaticOnly -StaticJobs 1 -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds 120 for the dedicated static lane and validate.ps1 -SkipStaticChecks -SkipTidySmoke for the Windows MSVC lane to avoid duplicate static validation; raw Linux, sanitizer, and macOS lanes pass explicit official --parallel job counts to CMake build and CTest. Windows vcpkg package/install caches and build caches restore with actions/cache/restore and attempt fresh non-blocking actions/cache/save writes only after validation succeeds. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1 when the game selects desktop-game-runtime, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 for the default sample-shell desktop-runtime-release lane, run tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget <target> for a registered desktop runtime package target with package smoke metadata and package file validation, run the explicit real-window D3D12 or Vulkan smoke command only on hosts with the matching runtime/toolchain support, and use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-editor.ps1 for the optional native Win32 first-party retained desktop editor shell lane; MK_editor_core remains covered by default validation.
